summary: Do not ship yet: the Autocomplete requirement degrades back to the browser-native UI on a visible loading-failure path and can disrupt keyboard focus during delayed loading.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Autocomplete fallback restores the UI the feature promises to remove
findings.body: The component begins loading Autocomplete only after the field subscribes, renders a native datalist until that import resolves, and resets the rejected promise without notifying or retrying for the currently mounted subscriber. A failed chunk request therefore leaves that form on the browser-dependent datalist indefinitely. On a delayed request, the eventual replacement also mounts a second input with autoFocus, which can reclaim focus after the user has moved to another field. The implementation explicitly anticipates chunk failures, while committed smoke evidence covers only successful normal-speed loading, so stories 4 and 13 are not protected under this visible degraded path.
findings.recommendation: Use a fallback that does not expose native datalist UI, preserve focus across the component transition without remount-driven autoFocus, retry or surface chunk-load failures for the mounted form, and add delayed/rejected-import tests proving focus is not stolen.

next_steps: Correct the Autocomplete loading and focus behavior.
Add delayed and rejected dynamic-import coverage, then rerun the committed release suite.
